title: glomeraria livida
intro: Glomeraria livida, also known as the Dead Man's Fingers or Coral Root, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This epiphytic plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Glomeraria livida is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant. The plant is often prepared for herbal use by drying the roots, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
